title: BLD-517: Switch hlx update from npm to GitHub main
description: Tour of changes to rewire the hlx CLI update system from npm-registry semver comparison to GitHub-main SHA-based comparison.

steps.title: GIT_INSTALL_SPEC replaces NPM_PACKAGE
steps.description: The npm package constant and functions (NPM_PACKAGE, fetchLatestVersion, isNewerVersion) are removed. A single GIT_INSTALL_SPEC constant is added, composed from existing CANONICAL_REPO_URL and CANONICAL_BRANCH constants. This is the canonical install spec used by perform.ts and error messages.

steps.title: Install spec switched to git URL
steps.description: The install spec now uses GIT_INSTALL_SPEC (git+https://...#main) instead of the npm registry package. npm is still the installer tool (it runs prepare/tsc and links the bin), but the npm registry is never contacted.

steps.title: Version output includes commit SHA
steps.description: getPackageVersion() now reads installSource.commit from the user's config. When a SHA is available, it returns '<semver> (<short-sha>)' (e.g., '1.3.4 (c8620a5)'). Falls back to semver-only gracefully on any config error.

steps.title: runUpdate() uses fetchRemoteSha() — fail-closed
steps.description: The explicit update command now fetches the remote main HEAD SHA via git ls-remote. On null return (network error), it exits non-zero immediately with a clear error message. This is the fail-closed behavior required by the ticket.

steps.title: Migration detection for npm-sourced installs
steps.description: When the install source is missing, mode 'npm', or mode 'unknown', a one-line notice is printed before proceeding with the GitHub-based install. This transparently migrates legacy npm users on their first 'hlx update'.

steps.title: Install source recorded as GitHub with SHA
steps.description: On successful update, saveConfig() records {mode: 'github', repo, branch, commit: remoteSha}. The commit field is the full 40-character SHA, used for future comparison and for the --version output.

steps.title: checkAutoUpdate() — fail-open with SHA comparison
steps.description: The pre-command auto-update check now uses SHA comparison. On network failure, it emits a stderr warning and returns silently — never blocking command dispatch. This is the fail-open behavior required by the ticket.

steps.title: --version handler with SHA fallback note
steps.description: When the version string doesn't contain a parenthesized SHA (legacy installs), a note is printed to stderr suggesting 'hlx update' to refresh install metadata.

steps.title: Documentation updated to git+https install URL
steps.description: The canonical install command in all documentation surfaces now references git+https://github.com/Project-X-Innovation/helix-cli.git#main instead of the npm registry package.
